title: capsicum indicum var. oliviforme
intro: Capsicum indicum var. oliviforme, also known as the Indian cherry pepper, is a variety of the Capsicum annuum plant, native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to prevent certain types of cancer. The Indian cherry pepper is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
